heading: Assessment of Corps of Engineers levees
label: 3307 Assessment of Corps of Engineers levees
notes: CodificationSection was enacted as part of the Water Resources Development Act of 2022, and also as part of the James M. Inhofe National Defense Authorization Act for Fiscal Year 2023, and not as part of the National Levee Safety Act of 2007 which comprises this chapter.
“Secretary” DefinedSecretary means the Secretary of the Army, see , set out as a note under .section 8002 of Pub. L. 117–263section 2201 of this title

children.text: Increasing the flood risk reduction benefits of such systems.
Achieving greater flood resiliency.

Restoring hydrological and ecological connections with adjacent floodplains that achieve greater environmental benefits without undermining flood risk reduction or flood resiliency for levee-protected communities.

children.heading: Levees operated by non-Federal interests
children.label: b Levees operated by non-Federal interests

children.text: requests the assessment; and
agrees to provide 50 percent of the cost of the assessment.

children.label: A
children.text: an estimate of the number of structures and population at risk and protected by the levee system that would be adversely impacted if the levee system fails or water levels exceed the height of any levee segment within the levee system (which may be the applicable estimate included in the levee database established under , if available);section 3303 of this title

children.label: B
children.text: the number of times the non-Federal interest has received emergency flood-fighting or repair assistance under for the levee system, and the total expenditures on postflood repairs over the life of the levee system;section 701n of this title

children.label: C
children.text: the functionality of the levee system with regard to higher precipitation levels, including due to changing climatic conditions and extreme weather events;

children.label: D
children.text: the potential costs and benefits (including environmental benefits and implications for levee-protected communities) from modifying the applicable levee system to restore connections with adjacent floodplains; and

children.label: E
children.text: available studies, information, literature, or data from relevant Federal, State, or local entities.

children.label: A
children.text: associated with an area that has been subject to flooding in two or more events in any 10-year period; and

children.label: B
children.text: for which the non-Federal interest has received emergency flood-fighting or repair assistance under with respect to such flood events.section 701n of this title

children.heading: Scope
children.label: 3 Scope
children.text: The Secretary shall ensure that an assessment under subsection (a) shall be similar in cost and scope to an initial assessment prepared by the Secretary pursuant to .section 549a of this title

children.heading: Flood plain management services
children.label: d Flood plain management services
children.text: In conducting an assessment under subsection (a), the Secretary shall consider information on floods and flood damages compiled under .section 709a of this title

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Not later than 18 months after , and periodically thereafter, the Secretary shall submit to the Committee on Transportation and Infrastructure of the House of Representatives and the Committee on Environment and Public Works of the Senate a report on the results of the assessments conducted under subsection (a).December 23, 2022

children.label: A
children.text: identification of any levee system for which the Secretary has conducted an assessment under subsection (a);

children.label: B
children.text: a description of any opportunities identified under such subsection for the modification of a levee system, including the potential benefits of such modification for the purposes identified under such subsection;

children.label: C
children.text: information relating to the willingness and ability of each applicable non-Federal interest to participate in a modification to the relevant levee system, including by obtaining any real estate necessary for the modification; and

children.label: D
children.text: a summary of the information considered and identified under subsection (c)(1).

children.heading: Incorporation of information
children.label: f Incorporation of information
children.text: The Secretary shall include in the levee database established under the information included in each report submitted under subsection (e), and make such information publicly available (including on a publicly available website).section 3303 of this title

children.heading: Levee system defined
children.label: g Levee system defined
children.text: In this section, the term “levee system” has the meaning given that term in .section 3301(9) of this title

children.heading: Authorization of appropriations
children.label: h Authorization of appropriations
children.text: There is authorized to be appropriated to carry out this section $10,000,000, to remain available until expended.
